input.prompt: My ASP.NET Core (.NET 8) app running on a Windows Server has slow requests under load. I have admin access. Which trace should I collect to capture waits/blocks, and what PerfView arguments should I use?

<current_datetime>2026-05-01T01:21:51.793Z</current_datetime>

My ASP.NET Core (.NET 8) app running on a Windows Server has slow requests under load. I have admin access. Which trace should I collect to capture waits/blocks, and what PerfView arguments should I use?

input.initialPrompt: My ASP.NET Core (.NET 8) app running on a Windows Server has slow requests under load. I have admin access. Which trace should I collect to capture waits/blocks, and what PerfView arguments should I use?

data.content:
## Collecting Thread-Time Stacks with PerfView

The trace you want is **Thread Time (with StartStop Activities)** — it captures both on-CPU and **off-CPU waits/blocks** (I/O, locks, thread-pool starvation).

### PerfView command

```cmd
PerfView.exe collect /ThreadTime /GCCollectOnly /NoGui /AcceptEula ^
  /MaxCollectSec:30 /CircularMB:1024 ^
  /Providers:"Microsoft-AspNetCore-Server-Kestrel,Microsoft-Extensions-Logging" ^
  /Zip:true /Merge:true
```

| Flag | Why |
|---|---|
| `/ThreadTime` | Enables context-switch tracing so you see **blocked time** (awaits, locks, sleep) |
| `/GCCollectOnly` | Lightweight GC data without expensive allocation tracking |
| `/CircularMB:1024` | Ring buffer — safe under sustained load |
| `/MaxCollectSec:30` | Auto-stops; extend if your repro takes longer |
| `/Providers:…` | Adds Kestrel + logging events for request correlation |

### Analysis

1. Open the `.etl.zip` → **Thread Time (with StartStop Activities)** view.
2. Group by `Process → Activity → Stack` to see per-request timelines.
3. Look for large **BLOCKED_TIME** frames — those reveal locks, async waits, or thread-pool queue delays.

> **Tip:** If you suspect thread-pool starvation specifically, also add `/Providers:"System.Threading.Tasks.TplEventSource:0x80:Informational"` to capture task/work-item queuing.
data.reasoningText: The user is asking about performance tracing for an ASP.NET Core (.NET 8) app on Windows Server, specifically about capturing waits/blocks under load using PerfView.
